commands.review_summary:
The PR reuses valid same-key untagged device tokens on metadata-refresh approvals, adds pairing-token regression coverage, and makes explicit local `openclaw devices` token/password calls use backend loopback auth without persisted CLI identity.

PR surface: Source +97, Tests +247. Total +344 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main always replaces role tokens during `approveDevicePairing`, so same-key metadata refresh approvals rotate the stored token. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Protected maintainer draft changes auth-token reuse and devices CLI loopback auth, so the next action is maintainer validation and final real-flow proof rather than an automated repair PR.

target.title: fix(pairing): preserve stable device tokens on metadata refresh
target.body:
## Summary
- preserve existing untagged device tokens for same-key metadata refresh approvals when the token was already valid before the approval
- keep rotating for scope expansion, public-key repair, stale baseline tokens, revoked/issuer-tagged tokens, incompatible role/scopes, and malformed persisted token entries
- add pairing token regression coverage for Windows node reconnect churn and unsafe reuse boundaries
- make `openclaw devices` use the direct local gateway-client admin path for explicit loopback token/password auth, avoiding stale paired CLI identity/scope traps

## Validation
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/device-pairing.test.ts`: 60 passed
- `pnpm test:serial src/cli/devices-cli.test.ts`: 46 passed
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`: clean, no accepted/actionable findings
- `OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=1 pnpm check:changed`: passed core/coreTests lanes in Blacksmith Testbox `tbx_01ksvg0w1cz5n3q882xxezpjzt` / run `26673710290`

## Notes
Draft because the full pond pairing / exec-approval validation against the signed Windows prerelease is still in progress.
target.author: vincentkoc
